name: State Street
cat: Financial Services / Asset Management and Custody

political.s: State Street demonstrates mixed political engagement with campaign contributions of $307,676 in 2024 and lobbying spending of $2.57 million in 2025. The company maintains a PAC that distributes to both parties, though exact partisan breakdown is unclear. A historical 2016 SEC case found the company engaged in a pay-to-play scheme involving illegal campaign contributions to win Ohio pension fund contracts.

charity.s: State Street Foundation focuses on education and workforce development with emphasis on racial equity and supporting disadvantaged populations. The foundation has invested over $8 million in Early College programs since 2020 and awarded $3.1 million in grants across a single quarter. Foundation assets total approximately $16.2 million with grantmaking spanning education, workforce development, and community services.
charity.focus: Education, Workforce Development, Racial Equity, Community Services

environment.s: State Street achieved environmental goals three years ahead of schedule, reducing greenhouse gas emissions and water use by 20 percent per person and diverting 90 percent of waste to landfills. However, recent proxy voting data shows significant reductions in environmental proposal support (6% in 2024 vs 14% in 2023), and the company continues to hold stakes in fossil fuel industries. The firm maintains ISO 14001 and 50001 certifications in select offices.

labor.s: In 2016, State Street launched the "Beacon" program cutting costs by shrinking the workforce to boost profits, and laid off 1,500 employees (increasing to 2,300 by July 2019). The company settled a 2017 lawsuit for $5 million over pay discrimination against female and Black executives. Labor relations data specific to NLRB complaints was not found in available sources.

dei.s: State Street historically championed DEI through its "Fearless Girl" initiative and pro-DEI policies, but reversed course in March 2025 by eliminating board diversity voting requirements following Trump administration pressure and political backlash. The company maintains internal DEI programs and employee networks but substantially downscaled external diversity advocacy messaging on its website.

animals.s: Not applicable - State Street is a financial services company with no involvement in animal testing or animal product manufacturing.

guns.s: Not applicable - State Street is a financial services and asset management company with no direct involvement in firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: State Street maintains detailed privacy policies across multiple jurisdictions and commits to responsible data handling with international data protection measures. The company encrypts secure portions of its websites and limits personal data collection to lawful purposes. However, the firm reserves the right to monitor and audit all transactions and communications for compliance purposes.

execPay.s: CEO Ron O'Hanley's 2024 compensation totaled $16.7 million (92.8% equity/bonus-based) at a 402:1 ratio to median employee pay. His compensation increased 24% year-over-year and exceeds industry median for similar-sized firms ($17M), though analysis suggests alignment with performance metrics and shareholder returns. The company shifted his incentive compensation entirely to equity-based vehicles in 2020.

fec.committeeName: TRUIST FINANCIAL CORPORATION FEDERAL, STATE & LOCAL POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (FORMERLY SUNTRUST BANKS, INC. MAIN STREET BANKERS PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
